explanation.takeaway: Top pair on overcard turn that brings villain range advantage = fold.

question.prompt: Flop 9d 8c 6h; turn Kc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ad Js?

explanation.short: AJ no pair no draw on K-overcard turn -- fold; six overcards are mostly dominated.
explanation.turnLogic: Kc turn does not help AdJs at all -- hero misses every flop card and has no straight or flush draw. Villain barrel range is range-advantaged + nut-advantaged on this runout.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AdJs on 9d 8c 6h was already thin (overcards only, no draw). Turn K shifts range hard to villain Kx.
explanation.handLogic: AdJs has no pair, no draw. Six overcard outs (3 aces + 3 jacks) but villain Kx range dominates Jx and many Ax pair outs lose to better Kings.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action and saves chips; raising as a bluff has near-zero fold equity vs Kx-anchored barrel range.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling AJ on overcard turns hoping to hit one of six outs ignores the mostly-dominated reality and burns chips.
explanation.takeaway: Naked overcards with no draw on overcard turn = fold; raise with no fold equity is a punt.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 6s 5s?

explanation.short: Made low flush on flush-completing turn -- call; raising bloats vs higher flushes.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; BTN barrel range now contains AsX/QsX higher flushes + air with no spade.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped low flush draw + small straight draw, called flop. Turn 2s makes flush; raising commits vs Ax-of-spades.
explanation.handLogic: 6s5s has made 6-high flush; only 1 card on river remaining so no further straight redraws apply.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air-bluffs without bloating vs nut flush. Small raise occasionally to charge worse flushes.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising any flush on monotone turns commits OOP into nut-flush range.
explanation.takeaway: Made low flush on flush-complete turn = call (raise small as alt; never big).

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-flush card; blocks every Ax-of-spades flush combo in BTN range.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Kd?

explanation.short: TPTK with nut-flush blocker on flush-complete turn -- call; nut blocker turns this into bluff-catcher.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; As blocks nut-flush combos and reduces villain barrel value range.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flat with AK pre, flop call with TPTK was easy. Turn flush threat looks scary, but As blocker = villain has no nut flush.
explanation.handLogic: AsKd has TPTK + As nut FD blocker + 2nd-nut redraw potential.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air; raising acceptable to charge K-flush combos but bloats OOP.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPTK on flush-complete turns when holding the As blocker over-folds vs villain barrel range.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK + nut-flush blocker on flush-complete turn = call (do not fold).

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-spade blocker AND gives hero the nut flush draw on river spades; hero removes every Ax-spade nut-flush combo from villain barrel range while retaining nut-flush redraw equity.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BB check-raises small with As Jd vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Nut spade blocker + nut FD redraw on flush-complete turn -- blocker check-raise (advanced; solver mixes).
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; As is the nut spade blocker (removes Ax-spade nut-flush combos) AND gives hero a nut-flush redraw on river spades. The raise mechanism is primarily blocker pressure, with nut-FD redraw as a secondary backup.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AsJd was natural (broadway high cards, A-blocker presence). Turn 2s makes the line a leveraged blocker check-raise with nut-flush redraw.
explanation.handLogic: AsJd has no current made hand or pair, but As provides (a) the nut spade blocker against villain Ax-spade combos, and (b) a nut flush redraw if river is a spade.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise size leverages the nut blocker; large raise risks too much when called by lower flushes.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as semi-bluff misses that the primary raise mechanism is the nut-spade blocker (the FD redraw is secondary backup, not the trigger). Identifying as value misses that hero has no made hand.
explanation.takeaway: Nut spade blocker + nut FD on flush-complete turn = blocker check-raise (advanced; mix-frequency line).

question.prompt: Flop Ks 8s 3d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Tc 9c?

explanation.short: No spade no draw on flush-complete turn -- fold; zero flush equity and dominated overcards.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush; without a spade, hero has no flush threat and faces a polarized barrel range (flushes + air-with-no-spade).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with T9 on K-high two-tone was already thin (overcards + backdoor potential only). Turn 2s eliminates the runner-runner equity and adds a flush hero cannot make.
explanation.handLogic: Tc9c has no spade, no pair, no current draw. Any straight would require Q+J on the runout (5-card 9-T-J-Q-K needs both Q and J), but only one card remains, so no straight is achievable. Pure give-up.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling on flush turn with zero flush equity is uniquely worst.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing turn with no spade on flush-completing turns is the prototypical leak.
explanation.takeaway: No spade + no draw on flush turn = fold.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9c 8h?

explanation.short: 8-9-T-J-Q straight on straight-complete turn -- raise for value.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes straight for 98 / KT / 89; BB flop call range with OESD + flush draws hits this turn hard. Range advantage swings to BB.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 98 (open-ender + flush draw) was strong; turn J makes straight. Villain barrel range still has Q-x / overpairs / TPTK that pay off raise.
explanation.handLogic: 98 makes 8-9-T-J-Q straight; only KT/T9 (not possible with 9 in hand) and KQ-flushes beat us (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise gets called by Q-x and overpairs; big raise also defensible vs nut-flush combos.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying turned straight on straight-complete turn surrenders huge value vs villain barrel range.
explanation.takeaway: Made straight on straight-complete turn = raise for value.

scenarios.blockerNote: K blocks K-high straight combos KT/K9; hero card is part of multiple straight structures.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kh Qh?

explanation.short: TPGK + OESD redraw to Broadway on straight-complete turn -- call.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes some lower straights but BB Q-x with redraw remains strong. Hero KhQh now has top pair (Q) plus an open-ended straight redraw: hero+board form T-J-Q-K (4 consecutive ranks), so 9 completes 9-T-J-Q-K and A completes Broadway T-J-Q-K-A. ~8 outs to a higher straight, with A giving the nut.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KQ on QT6 was natural (TPGK + backdoor). Turn J adds an OESD redraw to a higher straight.
explanation.handLogic: KhQh has TPGK + OESD redraw to higher straight (9 makes 9-T-J-Q-K, A makes Broadway T-J-Q-K-A).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air-bluffs + leaves the OESD redraw alive. Small raise also acceptable.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPGK + OESD redraw vs straight-complete turn over-folds vs villain barrel.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK + OESD redraw to higher straight on straight-complete turn = call.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BB calls with Tc 9d vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: T9 with double-gutshot + middle pair on straight-complete turn -- call to realize equity.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes some straights but T9 still has equity: pair of T + double-gutshot to 8 (8-9-T-J-Q) and K (9-T-J-Q-K).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with mid pair + gutshot was thin; turn J adds OE redraw potential.
explanation.handLogic: Tc9d has middle pair (T) + 8 outs to straight (need 8 for 8-9-T-J-Q or K for 9-T-J-Q-K).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes the multi-source equity at minimum cost. Raising too thin OOP without made hand stronger.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding the double-gutshot + pair under-defends vs villain barrel range that has air.
explanation.takeaway: Pair + double-gutshot on straight-complete turn = call; raise too thin.

question.prompt: Flop Qs Ts 6d; turn Jc. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 5h 4d?

explanation.short: 54 no pair no draw on straight-complete turn -- fold; bottom-of-flop range has no equity post-J.
explanation.turnLogic: Jc completes higher straights for villain barrel range (KT/89 etc.); 54 had backdoor potential on flop, killed by J.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 54 on Q-T-6 two-tone was thin (gutshot to 7 for 4-5-6-7-8 only); turn J kills it.
explanation.handLogic: 5h4d has no pair, no draw, no blocker; backdoor that died on the J.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling commits with zero equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing weak floats through straight-completing turns is uniquely worst.
explanation.takeaway: Bricked low float on straight turn = fold.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ah 3d?

explanation.short: Trip 3 paired turn = full house (3s full of 8s) -- slowplay; bluff range alive.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h pairs the 3 making BB trips into full house. BTN barrel range now contains overpairs / Ax-air / 88 (rare boats).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with A3o (likely thin float pre but consistent) -- turn 3 makes full house.
explanation.handLogic: Ah3d has full house 3s over 8s; only 88 (improbable) beats. Loses nothing on river.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain bluffs for river barrel; raising folds out air.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising boat on paired-paired turns folds bluffs that would barrel river.
explanation.takeaway: Full house on board-pairing turn = slowplay (do not raise).

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 5h 5d?

explanation.short: Pocket 5s on double-paired turn -- bluff-catch; pair beats air despite counterfeit risk.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h pairs board so hero plays 8-8-3-3 + 5 = two pair with 5 kicker. Counterfeited overpair to 3 but still beats every air-barrel.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 55 on 88-3 was bluff-catch territory; turn 3 keeps bluff catcher status (pair of 5 kicker + board two pair).
explanation.handLogic: 5h5d has pocket fives; final hand 8-8-3-3-5 beats all air, loses to 88 / overpairs / Ax with 3.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain air; raising folds bluffs and isolates better hands.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding small pocket pair on counterfeit-paired turns over-folds vs villain bluff-heavy range.
explanation.takeaway: Counterfeited underpair on double-paired turn = call (do not fold to "scary turn").

scenarios.blockerNote: A and K block AA / KK overpair value combos.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BB calls with Ad Kc vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: AK no pair on double-paired turn -- bluff-catch; A blocks villain top of range.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h double-pairs board; villain barrel range has overpairs + air. AK with Ace blocker beats all air.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AK on 88-3 was either thin float or blocker bet candidate. Turn 3 keeps bluff catch status.
explanation.handLogic: AdKc has no pair, no draw, but A blocks AA top-of-range and 6 overcard outs (river A or K).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures bluffs; raising folds them out; folding under-defends.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as range_disadvantage_turn_fold misses the A-blocker bluff-catch line.
explanation.takeaway: AK on counterfeit-paired turn = bluff-catch (call), not fold.

question.prompt: Flop 8c 8d 3s; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Qh Jh?

explanation.short: QJ no pair no draw on double-paired turn -- fold; no equity even on counterfeit board.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h double-pairs board. QJ has 6 overcard outs but pair of Q or J still loses to overpairs / Ax with 3 / 88.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with QJ on 88-3 was already thin (no pair, no draw, no relevant blocker); turn 3 keeps the situation bad.
explanation.handLogic: QhJh has no pair, no draw, no blocker. 6 pair outs are dominated.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Floating QJ "because two overcards" on paired boards leaks chips.
explanation.takeaway: Naked QJ on paired board after turn pair = fold.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Tc Td?

explanation.short: Top set T on draw-intensifier turn -- raise for protection AND value.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h adds 7 + 8 to straight-completing rivers (5-6-7-8-9 or 6-7-8-9-T). Many rivers kill action; raise now charges draws.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped top set; turn 6 adds new straight draws to villain range (78s) and BB needs to charge.
explanation.handLogic: TcTd makes top set; loses only to 99/55 sets and J-87 / 89 / 7-8 straights (rare flop call combos).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges OE/gutshots without folding K-x air entirely; big raise also defensible.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top set on draw-heavy turns lets villain realize equity for free.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on draw-intensifier turn = raise for protection.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 8c 7c?

explanation.short: Made straight 6-7-8-9-T on draw-intensifier turn -- raise for value.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h completes straight for 87/89; BB flop call with 87 OE was strong, turn 6 makes hand.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped strong combo (OE + flush draw); turn 6 makes nut straight on a dynamic board.
explanation.handLogic: 87 makes 6-7-8-9-T straight; only 8-7 (us) and J-x straight (J7-9 = 7-8-9-T-J needs J) compete (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges Tx + overpairs + draws; big raise defensible vs higher draws.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying turned straight on draw-heavy turn lets villain realize equity.
explanation.takeaway: Made straight on draw-intensifier turn = raise for value.

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-flush blocker on spades.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As 6s?

explanation.short: Nut FD + pair of 6 + Ace overcard on draw-intensifier turn -- call to realize multi-source equity.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h pairs hero turn card; nut FD still alive (4 spades visible: Ts, 9s, As, 6s -- 1 more spade on river makes nut flush). Hero now has pair of 6s plus the As overcard outs.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flopped nut FD + Ace overcard; turn 6 pairs hero (mid pair) and the board becomes more dynamic.
explanation.handLogic: As6s has nut FD (~9 spade outs to nut flush) + pair of 6 (~5 outs to two-pair/trips) + 3 Ace overcard outs + nut spade blocker. Multi-source equity, ~50%+ to barrel range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes ~50% equity cheaply. Raising too thin OOP given turn deepens pot more than flop.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding combo equity OOP under-defends vs villain barrel.
explanation.takeaway: Combo draw + pair on dynamic turn = call to realize equity.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 9s 5d; turn 6h. BB folds with Kh Qd vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: KQ no spade with weak gutshot on draw-intensifier turn -- fold; gutshot equity is dominated and there is no FD.
explanation.turnLogic: 6h adds new draws to villain range; BB KhQd has no spade FD and only a 4-out gutshot to J for 9-T-J-Q-K, which is dominated by villain Tx made hands and AK ranges.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KQ on T-9-5 two-tone was thin (gutshot to J + backdoor potential). Turn 6h preserves the gutshot but adds villain draws and reduces relative equity.
explanation.handLogic: KhQd has a 4-out gutshot to J (completes 9-T-J-Q-K) but no FD, no pair, no blocker. Pair outs (K or Q on river) are dominated by Kx/Qx in villain range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as bluff_catch_turn misses that no pair / no FD / no blocker has no bluff-catch claim.
explanation.takeaway: KQ no equity no blocker on dynamic turn = range_disadvantage fold (not bluff-catch).

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero turned OESD: 4-5-6-7 (4 consecutive) needs 3 OR 8 for 5-card straight = 8 outs.

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 6d 5d?

explanation.short: OESD on brick A-high turn -- call to realize 8-out straight equity at small price.
explanation.turnLogic: 4h is a brick for villain Ax range but completes hero's straight draw structure: hero+board ranks 2,4,5,6,7,A form 4-5-6-7 (4 consecutive). 3 or 8 makes the straight = 8 outs.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 6-5 suited on A-7-2 was thin (gutshot to 3-4-5-6-7 + BDFD). Turn 4 promotes to OESD; small barrel sizing gives clean pot odds.
explanation.handLogic: 6d5d has OESD (need 3 or 8) + bottom-end straight redraw via runner-runner. ~16% one-card equity matches small-bet breakeven.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity cheaply. Small check-raise also defensible as semi-bluff vs over-bluffy villain.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding OESD vs small barrel under-defends the BB calling range.
explanation.takeaway: OESD on brick A-high turn = call (pot odds + equity).

scenarios.blockerNote: No straight draw: hero+board ranks 2,4,6,7,A leave a 2-card gap (3 and 5 both needed) for 3-4-5-6-7. One-card gutshot does NOT exist.

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 7s 6h?

explanation.short: Middle pair 7 with no draw on brick turn -- call as bluff-catcher vs polarized barrel.
explanation.turnLogic: 4h is a brick. Hero+board ranks are 2,4,6,7,A: no one-card straight is available (3-4-5-6-7 needs both 3 and 5; 4-5-6-7-8 needs both 5 and 8). Hero has middle pair 7 with no draw equity, only showdown value vs villain bluffs.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 7-6o on A-7-2 was natural (middle pair). Turn 4 brick keeps hero at middle pair with no draw added.
explanation.handLogic: 7s6h has middle pair (7) with 6 kicker. Beats villain air and worse pairs (66, 55, 33, 22 if any). Loses to Ax, 77, sets, two-pair. Calling captures villain bluff frequency.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes pair showdown value vs polarized barrel. Raising thin pair OOP folds out bluffs and isolates against value.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding middle pair vs small barrel under-defends the BB calling range and pays off villain over-bluffing.
explanation.takeaway: Middle pair no draw on brick turn = call (bluff-catch).

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9c 9d?

explanation.short: Pocket 9s as underpair on A-high brick turn -- mixed call/fold (genuine indifference vs bluff frequency).
explanation.turnLogic: 4h does not change ranges meaningfully: hero remains underpair to A and 7. Vs villain barrel range (Ax value + air), pocket 9s is bluff catcher BUT below the threshold for many sizing/villain combos.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 99 vs BTN small c-bet on A-7-2 was bluff-catch territory. Turn 4 brick keeps the spot exactly the same -- a marginal pair vs a polarized barrel.
explanation.handLogic: 99 has 5 outs to set + beats villain air. Calling captures bluffs; folding avoids paying off Ax. Frequency depends on villain bluff %.
explanation.sizingLogic: Pure call vs over-bluffy villain; pure fold vs nitty villain. Solver mixes ~50/50 vs balanced barrel range.
explanation.commonMistake: Marking this as critical-fold or critical-call over-confident; it is a true mixed spot.
explanation.takeaway: Underpair on brick turn vs balanced range = mixed call/fold.

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kd Jc?

explanation.short: KJ no pair no draw on A-high brick -- fold; pair outs are dominated by Ax range.
explanation.turnLogic: 4h is a brick. Hero KJ has 6 overcard outs (3 K + 3 J) but villain barrel range is Ax-heavy: any K-pair on river loses to AK; any J-pair loses to AJ.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KJo on A-7-2 was already thin (no pair, no draw, no relevant blocker). Turn 4 keeps the situation hopeless.
explanation.handLogic: KdJc has zero made-hand value, no flush draw, no straight draw. The 6 overcard outs are dominated.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing KJ vs A-high barrels because of "two overcards" is the prototypical M4 leak.
explanation.takeaway: Naked broadway + dominated overcards on A-high brick = fold.

scenarios.blockerNote: A-blocker reduces villain AA value combos; Qc avoids board-turn Qh collision.

question.prompt: Flop 8d 6c 3s; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ah Qc?

explanation.short: TPTK on BB-favored Q overcard turn -- check-raise small for value.
explanation.turnLogic: Qh on 8-6-3 favors BB: BTN with Q-high range typically 3-bets preflop (AQ/KQ/QQ), so BTN barrel range is short on Qx value. BB call range has more Qx (suited Qx, KQs, AQs sometimes flat). Hero AhQh = TPTK and BB is value-favored on this turn.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AhQh on 8-6-3 with backdoor heart was natural. Turn Q makes TPTK; range advantage swings to BB.
explanation.handLogic: AhQh has TPTK with A kicker. Loses only to QQ (rare since BTN often 3-bets), 88/66/33 sets (rare flop call combos).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise charges weaker Qx (KQ, JQ if any), overpairs (TT-99 turning into bluff catcher), and air bluffs that fold. Big raise risks too much vs sets.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying TPTK on BB-favored overcard turn surrenders value vs villain Qx call-down.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK on BB-favored overcard turn = check-raise small for value.

question.prompt: Flop 8d 6c 3s; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Jd Td?

explanation.short: JT gutshot to 9 for 8-9-T-J-Q on Q overcard turn -- call (pot odds).
explanation.turnLogic: Qh adds top-of-range value for BB but also creates a gutshot for hero JT: hero+board has 8,T,J,Q + need 9 for 8-9-T-J-Q straight = 4 outs. Plus 6 overcard outs (3 K, 3 A blocked) -- some pair outs are dominated.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JTs on 8-6-3 was natural (overcards + BDFD). Turn Q creates the gutshot.
explanation.handLogic: JdTd has gutshot to 9 (4 outs) + 3 K-overcard outs (J-pair on K turn beats some bluffs) + slim runner-runner heart FD now dead.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes ~10% equity vs ~25% breakeven plus implied odds on straight completion.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding JT with new gutshot vs over-bluffy villain under-defends BB range.
explanation.takeaway: Gutshot + overcards on Q turn = call (pot odds + implied odds).

question.prompt: Flop 8d 6c 3s; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 6h 5h?

explanation.short: Middle pair 6 + 5 kicker on Q overcard turn -- fold; pair has been demoted.
explanation.turnLogic: Qh adds Qx to villain barrel range (some QJ/QT). Hero 6-pair was already weak on flop; Q overcard demotes it further -- now 6-pair sits below Q-pair, J-pair, T-pair, 8-pair AND has dominated kicker outs.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 6-5s on 8-6-3 was thin (mid pair + BDFD heart). Turn Q kills the BDFD (no heart) and shifts villain barrel range upward.
explanation.handLogic: 6h5h has middle pair 6 with 5 kicker. 5 outs to two-pair/trips but vs villain Qx/88/overpairs is far behind.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling realizes minimal equity in a range-disadvantaged spot.
explanation.commonMistake: Stationing weak middle pair vs overcard turn that adds villain value combos is a real leak.
explanation.takeaway: Demoted weak middle pair on overcard turn = fold (range disadvantage).

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8c 4s; turn Ah. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kh Qh?

explanation.short: TPGK demoted to mid pair on Ace overcard turn -- fold; K-pair is now dominated by Ax range.
explanation.turnLogic: Ah brings every Ax in BTN barrel range (AK / AQ / AJ / AT / AA). Hero K-pair was top pair on flop but is now SECOND pair below the A. Vs Kx-and-down hero is ahead but those rarely barrel.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KQ on K-8-4 was easy (TPGK). Turn A demotes the pair and shifts range hard to BTN.
explanation.handLogic: KhQh has middle pair (K, below Ace) with Q kicker. Vs Ax range hero is way behind; vs air hero beats it but villain rarely barrels air on A turn.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling stations into a dominated range.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing K-pair on A-overcard turn because "I had top pair on the flop" ignores the range shift.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK demoted by Ace overcard = fold (domination + range disadvantage).

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero blocks 88-cooler combos (only 1 eight remains).

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8c 4s; turn Ah. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 8d 8h?

explanation.short: Set of 8s on K-then-A turn -- check-raise small for value AND protection.
explanation.turnLogic: Ah brings every Ax in BTN barrel range to the table (now Ax is ahead of single-pair hands but behind sets). Hero set of 8s loses only to AA + KK (very rare). Many rivers (Q, J, T paired turns, runner-runner draws) kill action -- raise NOW while villain has Kx+Ax to call.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 88 on K-8-4 made middle set. Turn A adds Kx-second-pair and Ax-second-pair to villain's range -- both are still way behind set 8s.
explanation.handLogic: 88 makes set on 8-paired board. Beats AK/AQ/AJ/Kx/Qx etc. Loses only to AA (1 combo blocked) and KK (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise charges Ax + Kx + air. Big raise on a dynamic turn also defensible since many rivers kill action.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying set on A-overcard turn surrenders value vs Ax that pays off raise.
explanation.takeaway: Set on overcard turn = check-raise small for protection + value.

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8c 4s; turn Ah. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Js Ts?

explanation.short: JT gutshot to Q for T-J-Q-K-A on K-A turn -- call (pot odds + implied odds).
explanation.turnLogic: Ah completes Broadway-shape: hero+board has T,J,K,A + need Q for T-J-Q-K-A straight (the nuts) = 4 outs. Hero blocked from improvements but the gutshot to nuts is high-value when it hits.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JTs on K-8-4 was natural (broadway overcards + BDFD spade). Turn A creates the nut gutshot.
explanation.handLogic: JsTs has gutshot to nut Broadway (4 outs to the absolute nuts). Implied odds when villain barrels into Q river are huge.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures the gutshot at small price. Folding ignores the nut redraw.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding gutshot to the nuts vs small barrel under-defends and gives up high implied odds.
explanation.takeaway: Nut gutshot on overcard turn = call (pot odds + nut implied odds).

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero holds the nut flush (As-high spade flush).

question.prompt: Flop Qs 8s 4d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As 9s?

explanation.short: Made nut flush on flush-complete turn -- check-raise small for value.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s makes the third spade on board completing flushes for hero (As+9s+Qs+8s+2s = 5 spades). Hero has the absolute nuts vs every villain barrel value (Qx, set, two-pair) and lower flushes.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AsXs on Q-8-4 with 2 spades was speculative (overcard A + nut FD); turn 2s lands the nut flush.
explanation.handLogic: As9s makes 5-card spade flush A-Q-9-8-2 = nut flush. Loses to nothing.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise charges Q-pair / set / lower flushes; big raise also defensible vs villain who calls big with K-flush or set.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying nut flush on monotone turn surrenders value vs villain who sees scary turn and shuts down on river.
explanation.takeaway: Nut flush on flush-complete turn = check-raise for value.

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut spade blocker AND gives hero the nut flush draw on river spades.

question.prompt: Flop Qs 8s 4d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Kc?

explanation.short: No pair + nut spade blocker + nut FD redraw on flush-complete turn -- call (bluff-catch).
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush. Hero has no made hand but As blocks every nut-flush combo + provides nut FD redraw on river spades. The line is bluff-catch + drawing-to-nuts hybrid.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AsKc on Q-8-4 was natural (nut FD + 6 overcard outs). Turn 2s makes the FD reach but unhelpful for current showdown.
explanation.handLogic: AsKc has no pair, but As blocks villain Ax-spade-flush combos AND provides 1-card nut FD on river. Vs villain barrel range that contains air, hero's blocker + redraw justifies the call.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain bluffs and keeps the nut FD alive. Folding over-folds vs villain bluff frequency.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding the As blocker on flush-complete turn over-folds vs villain barrel range.
explanation.takeaway: Nut blocker + nut FD on flush turn = call (bluff-catch + redraw).

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero blocks 88 cooler set; only 1 eight left in deck.

question.prompt: Flop Qs 8s 4d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 8d 8h?

explanation.short: Middle set on flush-complete turn -- slowplay call (raising bloats vs flush).
explanation.turnLogic: 2s completes flush. Hero set is way ahead of villain Qx / overpairs / air but BEHIND any villain hand with 2 spades (made flush). Raising commits chips into flush range.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 88 on Q-8-4 was easy (middle set). Turn 2s makes flush threat real.
explanation.handLogic: 8d8h makes set of 8s + boat draw to 8 or river-pair. Loses to AsXs flushes + KsXs / JsXs etc. Set has ~4 outs to boat (any pair on river).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain bluffs for river. Raising small acceptable to charge worse hands but exposes to flush check-raise. Folding wastes the set value vs villain's air bucket.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising set on monotone turns commits OOP into flush range.
explanation.takeaway: Set on flush-complete turn = slowplay call (do not raise into flush).

scenarios.blockerNote: Hero holds the nut straight 7-8-9-T-J on a BB-favored straight-complete turn.

question.prompt: Flop 9s 8d 4c; turn 7h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Jh Th?

explanation.short: Made NUT straight 7-8-9-T-J on BB-favored straight-complete turn -- check-raise BIG for max value.
explanation.turnLogic: 7h completes straight for several BB-range hands (T6, 65, 87, JT etc.). BB is range-favored on this turn (BTN with JT/T6 typically does NOT 3-bet; BB call range catches these straights). BTN barrel range is overpair-heavy + air -- both pay off bigger sizing on a polar turn.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JTs on 9-8-4 with backdoor heart was natural (overcards + BDFD + gutshot to 6 or J-T-9-8-? actually JT on 984 has gutshot to 7 already on the flop which lands turn). Turn 7 makes nut straight.
explanation.handLogic: JhTh makes 7-8-9-T-J = J-high straight = nuts. Beats every villain hand. Even higher straights cannot exist (would need K-Q on board).
explanation.sizingLogic: BIG check-raise extracts max from villain overpairs, top-pair, sets that may continue. Small raise leaves value behind on a BB-favored polar turn.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying the nut straight on a BB-favored polar turn surrenders value vs villain who is forced to defend with overpairs/sets.
explanation.takeaway: Nut straight on BB-favored polar turn = check-raise BIG (rare but justified spot).

question.prompt: Flop 9s 8d 4c; turn 7h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ah Ad?

explanation.short: AA overpair on BB-favored straight-complete turn -- mixed call/fold (range-disadvantaged but still beats bluffs).
explanation.turnLogic: 7h completes straights for many BB-range hands (T6, 65, 87, JT, 56). AA is now BEHIND every made straight but AHEAD of overpairs lower than itself + air. BTN barrel range polarized -- some KK/QQ paying off + air bluffs continuing.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AA vs BTN small c-bet (sometimes flat-traps overpair to mask range). Turn 7 polarizes the spot heavily.
explanation.handLogic: AhAd has overpair + nothing else. Vs barrel: ahead of TT/JJ overpairs that turn into bluff catchers + air; behind every straight.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain bluffs/lower-overpairs. Folding avoids paying off straights. The math splits depending on villain frequency. Pure call vs over-bluffy; pure fold vs nitty.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-calling AA into a polar turn that completes draws ignores how scary the runout is. Auto-folding ignores bluff frequency.
explanation.takeaway: Overpair on BB-favored straight turn = mixed call/fold (true indifference).

question.prompt: Flop 9s 8d 4c; turn 7h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kd Jd?

explanation.short: KJ gutshot to T for 7-8-9-T-J on BB-favored straight turn -- call (pot odds).

explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KJs on 9-8-4 was thin (overcards + BDFD diamond). Turn 7 adds the gutshot.
explanation.handLogic: KdJd has gutshot to T (4 outs to second-nut-straight) + slim overcard equity. Vs barrel that contains air, multi-source equity earns the small call.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity cheaply with implied odds when straight hits.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding new-gutshot vs small barrel under-defends BB range.

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8s 3c; turn 8h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ad Kh?

explanation.short: TPTK on board-paired (Kx + 8x paired) turn -- call (bluff-catch).
explanation.turnLogic: 8h pairs the second board card making board K-8-8-3. Hero TPTK is still ahead of villain's pair-of-K and air. Loses to KK (rare), 88 (3 combos), 33 (3 combos), Ax with 3 (but rare flop call). Most barrel range = bluff or pair-of-K-weaker.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AKo on K-8-3 was easy (TPTK). Turn 8 paired board doesn't change relative ranking.
explanation.handLogic: AdKh has TPTK + A blocker. Beats Kx-with-weaker-kicker + bluffs. Loses to KK / 88 / 33 / 8x with pair (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures bluffs. Raising folds bluffs and isolates against KK/8x-trip.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding TPTK on paired board because "trip 8 might exist" over-folds vs villain bluff frequency.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK on board-paired turn = call (bluff-catch).

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8s 3c; turn 8h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ks Kc?

explanation.short: Top set on K then turn pairs board to 8-8 = full house Kings full of 8s -- check-raise BIG for max value.
explanation.turnLogic: 8h pairs board (K-8-8-3) turning hero's top set into a near-nut full house (only 88 quads beats hero, virtually impossible since only 1 eight left and villain rarely has 88 in barrel range).
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KK vs BTN open is unusual but possible (slow-flat to mask range / disguise on K-high boards). On turn 8 paired = hero has K-K-K-8-8 = top boat.
explanation.handLogic: KsKc + K-8-8-3 board = Kings full of 8s = full house. Beats every Kx, every 8x trip, every smaller boat. Loses only to 88 quads.
explanation.sizingLogic: BIG check-raise extracts max from villain who pays off with Kx, 8x trips, AA/QQ overpairs hoping to bluff-catch. Small raise leaves value behind on a paired board where villain knows hero range is polar.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top boat on paired turn surrenders huge value vs villain who is calling bigger sizes with Kx + 8x trips.
explanation.takeaway: Top boat on board-paired turn = check-raise BIG for max value.

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8s 3c; turn 8h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Qh Jh?

explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with QJs on K-8-3 with backdoor heart was thin. Turn 8 paired keeps the situation hopeless for naked broadway.
explanation.handLogic: QhJh has zero made-hand value, no straight draw, no flush draw (hearts dead since only 1 heart on board). Pair outs dominated.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing QJ vs K-paired turn because of two overcards is the prototypical M4 leak.
explanation.takeaway: Naked broadway on board-paired turn = fold (domination).

question.prompt: Flop Ts 8s 4d; turn 7c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Ks?

explanation.handLogic: AsKs has nut FD (~9 spade outs) + 6 overcard outs (some dominated by A/K-pair villain hands but mostly clean). Multi-source equity earns the call vs barrel.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 8s 4d; turn 7c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 9c 8c?

explanation.turnLogic: 7c adds OESD: hero+board has 7,8,9,T (4 consecutive) + need J or 6 for straight = 8 outs. Plus pair of 8 (board 8) = additional outs to two-pair/trips. Plus club backdoor flush draw.

explanation.handLogic: 9c8c has pair of 8 + OESD (J or 6) + 4 outs to two-pair (any 9) + slim BDFD = ~14 outs total. Strong combo equity.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise leverages fold equity vs villain weaker overpairs (TT-99) + air, while strong made hands continue. Big raise risks too much given hero is not yet made.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as protection misses the SEMI-BLUFF nature: hero has fold equity AND draw equity.
explanation.takeaway: Strong combo (pair + OESD) on dynamic turn = semi-bluff check-raise.

explanation.turnLogic: 7h adds OESD: hero+board has 4,7,8,9,T,T = 7-8-9-T four consecutive + need 6 or J for straight = 8 outs, plus 2 outs to set of Ts and pair-of-board outs. The 7 also turns BB range into the value-favored side, so villain barrel range is more polarized.

The PRIMARY reason is BLOCKER pressure, not semi-bluff -- semi_bluff_check_raise_turn is acceptable but secondary because the made-hand-replacement value is the blocker, not the draw.

explanation.turnLogic: 7c lands the connector that completes multiple straights on T-8-4: 65 makes 4-5-6-7-8, 96 makes 6-7-8-9-T, J9 makes 7-8-9-T-J. Plus 98 has pair-of-8 + OESD and T9 has top pair + OESD. The made-straight density in villain barrel range is significant; QQ is now overpair-to-T but BEHIND every made straight + every set, sitting in bluff-catch territory only.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with QQ on T-8-4 was natural overpair. Turn 7 polarizes villain barrel: the value side now contains made straights (65/96/J9) and sets/two-pair; the bluff side contains failed broadway gutshots and missed overcards. QQ does not interact with any of the straight-completing combos, so blocker effects are minimal.
explanation.handLogic: QcQd has overpair below only KK/AA. Beats all Tx pairs, all 8x pairs, all underpairs, all bluffs and air. Loses to 65/96/J9 made straights, TT/88/44 sets, T8/T4/84 two-pair (rare combos in BTN flop call range).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures the air/bluff portion of the polarized barrel range and reaches showdown. Small check-raise folds out air and isolates against the straight portion that crushes QQ -- bad EV. Big raise even worse. Folding is too tight given QQ still beats every bluff.
explanation.commonMistake: Hard-coding overpair = "always raise for protection on draw turns" without checking which DRAWS actually completed leads to raising into completed-straight density. The 7c here is straight-complete, not draw-intensifier.
explanation.takeaway: Overpair on straight-complete turn vs polar barrel = call (bluff-catch); not protection-raise.

scenarios.blockerNote: Made 9-high straight 5-6-7-8-9 on a monotone-club turn. Hero holds ZERO clubs (7s5s = both spades) so has NO flush blocker; loses to every made flush (any Ax-clubs, any 2-club hand) and to T-high straight (T7 specifically; 6-7-8-9-T).

question.prompt: Flop 9c 6c 3h; turn 8c. BB calls with 7s 5s vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Bottom straight 5-6-7-8-9 on flush-complete monotone turn with NO flush blocker -- call (bluff-catch); raising loses to flushes + T7.
explanation.turnLogic: 8c lands the 9-high straight AND completes a 3-card club flush. Villain barrel range on this monotone-flush-complete turn polarizes toward made flushes (any 2-club hand: Ax-clubs nut flush, KQ-clubs, JT-clubs, etc.) plus the higher T-high straight (T7) plus air bluffs (missed overcards / failed gutshots). Hero straight is BEHIND every made flush and behind T7; ahead of every set, every two-pair, every pair, every air combo.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 75-suited on 9-6-3 was thin (gutshot to 8 + backdoor flush via spades). Turn 8c lands the straight but kills the spade BDFD (wrong suit) and simultaneously completes the club-flush threat against hero.
explanation.handLogic: 7s5s makes 5-6-7-8-9 straight (the LOWEST possible straight on this board). Loses to: every made flush (Ax-clubs, KQ-clubs, 2-club combos -- significant density on monotone turn), T7 made T-high straight (6-7-8-9-T). Beats: every set (66/99/33/88), every two-pair, every pair, every air bluff, every weaker straight (none lower exists).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures the air/bluff portion of the polarized barrel and reaches showdown without bloating vs flushes. Small check-raise is bad EV: it folds out the air bucket (which we beat) and isolates against flushes + T7 (which crush us). Mixed defensible vs sizing/opponent. Big raise commits chips against a value range we mostly lose to.
explanation.commonMistake: Reading made-straight as "always-value-raise" without checking the flush-complete texture or the higher-straight threat (T7) leads to raising into a value range that beats us. Bottom straight on monotone turn with no suit blocker is bluff-catch, not value-raise.
explanation.takeaway: Bottom straight on monotone-flush-complete turn with no suit blocker = call (bluff-catch); not value-raise.

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BB folds with 9d 8d vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: No pair no live draw on A-high brick turn -- fold; BB range is structurally disadvantaged here.
explanation.turnLogic: 4h is a brick. Villain A-high barrel range is unchanged from flop. Hero 9d8d has no pair; the diamond backdoor is alive but very thin (3 diamonds total: 7d on board plus hero 9d 8d -- runner-runner flush only). No live straight draw: hero 8-9 plus board 2-4-7-A produces zero one-card-straight outs.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 98-suited on A-7-2 was thin (backdoor flush + backdoor straight reach). Turn 4 leaves only the runner-runner backdoor diamond flush; the supposed straight reach evaporates because no single river card makes hero a five-card straight.
explanation.handLogic: 9d8d has no pair, no one-card draw (zero gutshot since hero+board ranks are 2,4,7,8,9,A with no five-rank window), only a runner-runner backdoor diamond flush. Effective equity vs villain barrel range is roughly 6-8%.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling pays a non-trivial price for runner-runner backdoor equity; semi-bluff raise commits chips on a board where BB range is structurally weak vs A-high barrel.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing weak runner-runner backdoors on A-high BB-disfavored turns because the call price is small ignores the underlying range disadvantage.
explanation.takeaway: Naked runner-runner backdoor on A-high brick turn = fold (range disadvantage; pot odds insufficient for thin backdoor equity).

question.prompt: Flop Qs 8s 4d; turn 2s. BB folds with Tc 9c vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Tc9c on flush-complete turn -- fold; the board changed against hero hand category.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s makes the 3rd spade and completes the flush. Hero has no spade in hand, no pair, no draw. The board change collapses hero equity: every spade combo in villain range now beats hero entirely; non-spade combos still beat T-high. Pre-turn hero had backdoor flush + backdoor straight -- both are now dead.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with T9-suited on Q-8-4-spade-spade was thin (overcards-to-8, backdoor straight, backdoor flush). Turn 2s kills BDFD (wrong suit) and BDSD (no straight reach via 2).
explanation.handLogic: Tc9c has no pair, no flush, no straight draw, no relevant blocker. Zero showdown value vs every made hand and most air on a flush-complete board.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated 6 overcards (T or 9 still loses to flushes); raising commits with no equity backbone vs villain made-flush combos.
explanation.commonMistake: Defending two overcards on flush-complete turns because of the small call price ignores the board change against hero range.
explanation.takeaway: Naked overcards on flush-complete turn = fold (board changed against hero category, not just range disadvantage).

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8s 3c; turn 8h. BB folds with 7c 7d vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Underpair 77 on board-paired K-high turn -- fold; dominated by 8x trips and Kx top pair.
explanation.turnLogic: 8h pairs the 8 and gives villain trip-8 combos (88/A8/K8/Q8). Hero 77 is underpair-below-the-second-board-pair (since the turned 8 is now its own pair-holding category) and crushed by Kx top pair plus 8x trips. The villain barrel range concentrates on Kx-strong-kicker plus turned 8x trips; underpair-to-K is dominated structurally.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 77 on K-8-3 was thin (underpair to top, ahead only of T9-air). Turn 8 doubles the second board card and adds trip combos to villain range.
explanation.handLogic: 7c7d has underpair to K, dominated by every Kx in villain range, every 8x trips combo (88/A8/K8/Q8), and all overpairs. Only 2 outs to set-of-7s.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling pays off Kx + 8x value with only 2 outs to set; raising commits chips into a dominated range with no fold-equity story (no relevant blocker).
explanation.commonMistake: Stationing underpairs vs board-pair turns because pot odds look small ignores the trips threat that fundamentally dominates the spot.
explanation.takeaway: Underpair on board-pair turn vs Kx-heavy barrel = fold (dominated, not just range disadvantage).

scenarios.blockerNote: As-blocker reduces villain AA combos. Spade BDFD via 9s; 9 also gives gutshot to 8 for 8-9-T-J + need Q? No: 9 + T-J on board = need 7-8 OR Q-K for straight; 9 alone does not give a one-card straight.

question.prompt: Flop Jd Td 5s; turn 2c. BB check-raises small with As 9s vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: As9s no pair on polar brick turn -- check-raise small (blocker bluff using A-blocker).
explanation.turnLogic: 2c is a polar brick. Villain barrel range is wedged: nutted Tx/Jx/two-pair/sets vs total air. Hero As9s has zero made-hand value but the A-blocker reduces villain AA value combos AND the polar brick combined with BB range advantage on J-T-5 lets hero credibly represent JT/TT/55 sets. The 9 adds modest backdoor straight reach; the spade BDFD is dead via the 2c.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with A9-suited on J-T-5 was natural (overcard A + backdoor flush + backdoor gutshot). Turn 2 brick changes nothing but unlocks a polar bluff line for hero.
explanation.handLogic: As9s has no made hand, no live FD (BDFD dead via 2c brick), only modest gutshot reach through future cards. The lesson is the BLOCKER + range structure, not equity.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise leverages BB range advantage on J-T-5 plus A-blocker; calling surrenders the line. Big raise risks too much without made-hand backbone.
explanation.commonMistake: Reading this as semi_bluff_check_raise (when BDFD is actually dead) inflates the equity story; the dominant motive is the BLOCKER + range advantage.
explanation.takeaway: A-blocker bluff on polar brick where BB range is favored = blocker_check_raise (not semi-bluff).

scenarios.blockerNote: Set of 5s on board where 5-6-7-8-9 straight already completed via any 6 in villain range. Hero set is the second-tier nutted region (loses to 6-x straights only).

question.prompt: Flop 9s 8d 4c; turn 7h. BB calls with 5h 5d vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Set of 5s on BB-favored straight-complete turn -- call to slowplay; raising chases the polarized barrel range air bucket.
explanation.turnLogic: 7h lands the 5-6-7-8-9 straight via any 6. Hero set of 5s is very strong (top portion of made-hand region) but raising charges only T+ value combos (rare on flop call) and folds out the entire bluff bucket immediately. Calling traps villain bluffs and lets villain barrel a third street with weaker hands.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 55 on 9-8-4 was natural (underpair + set draw). Turn 7 lands the straight on the BB-favored side and brings villain barrel range toward polar (T-high made or air).
explanation.handLogic: 5h5d makes set of 5s. Loses only to 6-x straight combos (rare on flop call: 65, 64s, A5-A4 wheel hands). Beats every weaker pair, every two-pair, all bluffs. Vs villain barrel, ahead of every air combo and every Tx weaker.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling slowplays and traps the bluff range. Small check-raise is value-raise but folds out the bluff bucket immediately, surrendering river barrel value. Big raise even worse.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising sets on polar straight-complete turns chases out the air range and surrenders river value.
explanation.takeaway: Set on BB-favored polar straight-complete turn vs polarized barrel = call (slowplay) -- not value-raise.

scenarios.blockerNote: Overpair AA with one A in each red suit; no flush blocker because flop+turn are 7s 5d 3h 4c (rainbow). Diamond-blocker reduces 6-of-diamonds river straight-completion combos slightly.

question.prompt: Flop 7s 5d 3h; turn 4c. BB plays a mixed strategy with Ah Ad vs BTN's turn barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Overpair AA on low BB-favored straight-complete turn -- mixed strategy: value-raise vs slowplay-call are both defensible.
explanation.turnLogic: 4c lands the bottom straight (3-4-5-6-7 needs 6). Hero AA overpair beats every made hand except 6-x straight combos. The mix tension: value-raising charges 7-x value-call combos but folds bluffs; slowplay-calling traps bluffs but lets sky-rivers (6) potentially redirect. On a BB-favored board where villain barrel range polarizes toward straight-value vs air, the EV-difference between raise-small and call is razor-thin.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AA on 7-5-3 was thin (overpair to all four board ranks, no draw). Turn 4 favors BB structurally but also brings made-straight redirection.
explanation.handLogic: AhAd has overpair AA, the absolute top non-set non-straight hand. Loses to 65/64/A5 wheel-hand straights (rare BTN combo on flop call); beats every 7x, every weaker pair, every air. Hero diamond-blocker is irrelevant since the board is rainbow.
explanation.sizingLogic: Mixed strategy reflects that small check-raise and call have similar EV: small raise extracts from 7x and air-bluffs but folds the air bucket immediately; calling traps bluffs and reaches the river with deception. Population/opponent tendencies tip the right strategy.
explanation.commonMistake: Hard-coding EITHER value-raise OR slowplay misses the indifference -- this is exactly where mixed reflects the math.
explanation.takeaway: Overpair on BB-favored polar straight-complete turn = mixed (the right strategy is opponent-and-sizing dependent).

scenarios.blockerNote: A-spade gives the nut flush draw (4 spades on board+hand) AND blocks villain nut-flush combos. But the 8d second-pair is dominated by every Qx made hand and most overpairs.

question.prompt: Flop Qs 8s 4d; turn 2s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As 8d?

explanation.short: A8 second-pair-with-NFD on flush-complete turn -- fold (call defensible with the NFD); second-pair is dominated and the FD just barely meets pot odds against a value-heavy barrel.
explanation.turnLogic: 2s makes the third spade. Hero As8d has nut FD via Ace-of-spades (need one more spade) plus second-pair (8) on Q-high. The NFD has live equity (~19% one-card) but second-pair is dominated by all Qx, every overpair, every Q-x two-pair, plus made flushes that include hero outs sometimes. Combined hot/cold equity is below the call threshold given range disadvantage.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with A8-offsuit on Q-8-4 with backdoor spade was thin (second-pair + BDFD via the A-spade card path). Turn 2s lands the FD but on a board where flush already completes -- meaningful only as nut-blocker not as primary equity.
explanation.handLogic: As8d has second-pair (8) plus NFD (need 1 more spade). Beats Tx-with-no-spade and air bluffs. Loses to all Qx, all overpairs, all made flushes (Ax-of-spades hero is the nut-flush-blocker but villain still has KQ-spades, JT-spades, etc).
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling pays off Qx + flushes too often given the 8-pair dominated portion; raising commits with no value and no clear bluff story (NFD is small fraction of hero range).
explanation.commonMistake: Calling A-x with NFD-redraw on flush-complete turns when the made-hand portion is dominated under-defends-value the BB range too thinly.
explanation.takeaway: Second-pair plus NFD on flush-complete turn = fold (dominated; FD not enough at small price + range disadvantage).

scenarios.blockerNote: K-pair with J kicker; K-blocker reduces KQ/KK combos slightly but Q overcard hits villain range hard. J also gives gutshot to T for K-Q-J-T-? but needs both T and 9-or-A on river.

question.prompt: Flop Kc 7s 2d; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Kh Jd?

explanation.short: KJ TPWK on overcard Q turn -- fold; Q overcard hits villain KQ-AQ value cluster and hero is dominated.
explanation.turnLogic: Qh is an overcard between hero K kicker and villain natural barreling range (KQ specifically). Hero K-pair-with-J-kicker is now dominated by every KQ in villain range (preflop standard 2.5x range includes KQ-suited and offsuit), every AK, every QQ overpair, plus AQ that picks up TP-with-A-kicker. Hero only beats K7/K2 (not in BTN preflop range), weaker Kx (rare), and air.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KJ-offsuit on K-7-2 was natural (TPWK with no draw). Turn Q changes the structure: villain barrel range now leverages KQ + AQ + AK significantly.
explanation.handLogic: KhJd has TPWK (K-pair, J kicker), gutshot to T-A or T-9 for K-Q-J-T-A (need T plus A or 9 in run-out -- low-effective-equity outs). Dominated by KQ, AK, AQ, KK, QQ. Beats K-low and air.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling pays off the KQ-AQ-AK cluster while only beating air; raising commits with a dominated weak-kicker top pair.
explanation.commonMistake: Stationing TPWK on overcard turns when the overcard hits villain barreling range hard ignores the structural domination.
explanation.takeaway: TPWK on overcard turn that hits villain range = fold (dominated, not just second-best).

scenarios.blockerNote: Gutshot to A makes broadway K-Q-J-T-A (4 outs to A); also gutshot to 9 makes K-Q-J-T-9 NOT broadway but gutshot variant does not exist here (hero has J-T, board K-Q-7-2; 9 in hand-or-board would not make a straight). Actually 9 makes 9-T-J-Q-K straight -- 4 outs. So total gutshot-to-A or 9 = 8 outs to two non-overlapping straights.

question.prompt: Flop Kc 7s 2d; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Jh Th?

explanation.short: JT broadway gutshot+gutshot OESD-equivalent on overcard Q turn -- call (multi-source equity + heart backdoor).
explanation.turnLogic: Qh adds K-Q-J-T two-card straight reach: any 9 makes 9-T-J-Q-K and any A makes T-J-Q-K-A. Combined 8 outs to two non-overlapping straights = ~17% one-card equity. Plus heart backdoor flush (Jh Th + Qh on board = 3 hearts; need 2 more for flush -- live runner-runner). Hero has overcards that may also pair to TP on river.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JTs on K-7-2 was thin (overcards + backdoor straight + backdoor flush via hearts). Turn Q lands the OESD-equivalent (two non-overlapping gutshots) directly.
explanation.handLogic: JhTh has 8 gutshot outs to broadway or 9-high straight, plus heart BDFD (3 hearts), plus possible J/T pair-to-river (6 overcard outs of which J-pair might still beat air). Combined ~22% equity vs villain barrel.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity at small price. Mixed defensible vs sizing/opponent. Folding combo equity OOP under-defends BB range. Big raise commits with insufficient made-hand backbone.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding double-gutshot-plus-BDFD vs small barrel under-defends the BB calling range and abandons real equity.
explanation.takeaway: Double-gutshot + BDFD on overcard turn = call (multi-source equity).

scenarios.blockerNote: A-spade blocks AA value combos and reduces nut-FD-on-future-spade-rivers. Plus broadway gutshot via J for A-K-Q-J-T (4 outs).

question.prompt: Flop Kc 7s 2d; turn Qh. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Ts?

explanation.short: AT broadway gutshot + A-blocker on overcard Q turn -- check-raise small (blocker bluff with backbone equity).
explanation.turnLogic: Qh adds A-K-Q-J-T broadway reach (need J for nut straight; 4 outs ~ 9% one-card). Hero As blocks AA value (rare BTN flop call) and the A-blocker plus K-on-board reduces villain AK-strong combos. The blocker plus 4 nut-straight outs makes the bluff line credible.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with ATs on K-7-2 was thin (overcards + backdoor straight). Turn Q gives the A-K-Q-J-T gutshot directly.
explanation.handLogic: AsTs has no pair, gutshot to nut straight (4 outs ~ 9%), A-blocker reducing AA combos. The bluff is supported by the blocker structure, not by raw equity.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise leverages A-blocker plus nut-straight redraw. Calling realizes equity passively. Folding gutshot+blocker under-defends. Big raise commits too much without a real made-hand or strong-equity backbone.
explanation.commonMistake: Reading this as semi_bluff_check_raise overstates the gutshot equity; the dominant motive is the A-blocker plus the nut-straight redraw together.
explanation.takeaway: Nut-straight gutshot + A-blocker on overcard turn = check-raise small (blocker_check_raise).

scenarios.blockerNote: A-spade gives the NUT flush draw on the 2-spade flop (Ts + 8s = 2 spades; plus hero 2 spades = 4 spades total). 9 outs to nut flush plus A and Q overcard pair outs.

question.prompt: Flop Ts 8s 4d; turn 7c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Qs?

explanation.short: AQ NFD on draw-intensifier turn -- check-raise small (semi-bluff with strong nut-equity backbone).
explanation.turnLogic: 7c expands draw density (FDs, OESDs, gutshots). Hero AsQs has nut FD via A-spade (need 1 more spade among 9 outs ~ 19%) plus overcard pair outs (A and Q over T-high board). The A-blocker also reduces villain AA value combos. Combined raw equity plus blocker dynamics support the semi-bluff line.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AQs on T-8-4 was natural (overcards + nut spade backdoor). Turn 7 reweights to draws-and-bluffs while hero gets the live NFD.
explanation.handLogic: AsQs has no made hand, 9 outs to nut flush, plus A/Q overcard pair outs (~6 outs vs villain Tx-weaker weak made-hand portion). ~25% raw equity vs villain barrel range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise leverages NFD equity + fold equity vs villain weak made-hand range (Tx-weak, 8x, weak overpairs). Calling passively realizes equity but surrenders fold-equity. Folding NFD+overcards vs small barrel under-defends.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding NFD vs small turn barrel because of "no pair" ignores the 9-out one-card nut flush draw equity that exceeds pot odds AND fold-equity backbone.
explanation.takeaway: NUT FD on draw-intensifier turn = check-raise small (semi-bluff, nut-equity backbone + A-blocker).

scenarios.blockerNote: TPTK with A-pair, K kicker. A-heart blocker reduces villain AA combos.

question.prompt: Flop Ac 7d 2s; turn 4h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ah Kc?

explanation.short: TPTK with A-blocker on A-high brick turn -- check-raise small for value.
explanation.turnLogic: 4h is a brick. Hero AhKc has TPTK on A-high disconnected dry board. Villain barrel range here is wide (Ax-weaker, mid pairs, occasional bluffs); raising charges Ax-weaker call-down and folds out air bluffs. The A-blocker further reduces villain AA + AK combos.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AK-offsuit on A-7-2 was natural (TPTK with no draw needed). Turn 4 brick adds nothing meaningful; hero range is favored on A-high dry boards relative to villain.
explanation.handLogic: AhKc has TPTK with K kicker plus A-blocker reducing AA combos. Beats AQ/AJ/AT/A-low, all underpairs, all air. Loses only to AA (rare with hero A-blocker), A7/A4/A2 two-pair (rare combo), 77/22/44 sets.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise charges weaker Ax and folds out air. Big raise risks too much vs the rare two-pair/set combos. Calling slowplays acceptable but surrenders value vs villain Ax call-down.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-calling TPTK on A-high brick turns when villain barrel range is wide surrenders chips that worse Ax pays off.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK with A-blocker on A-high brick = check-raise small for value.

scenarios.blockerNote: Set of tens on draw-heavy turn. Loses only to T8/T4/T7 turned two-pair (rare BTN flop call combos) and 9-6/J-9 made straights (J-9 specifically makes 7-8-9-T-J straight -- one combo).

question.prompt: Flop Ts 8s 4d; turn 7c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Tc Td?

explanation.short: Top set on draw-intensifier turn -- check-raise BIG for value to charge draws maximum.
explanation.turnLogic: 7c expands draw density (FDs, OESDs, gutshots). Hero set of tens is the absolute top of the made-hand region for BB on T-8-4-7. Villain barrel range now includes many strong draws (KsQs/JsQs/9c8c-style combos) that call big sizing because they have ~30%+ equity. Big raise extracts maximum from draws AND from worse made hands that may stack off.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with TT on T-8-4 was natural (top set, slowplay candidate flopwise). Turn 7 makes raising MORE attractive because draws now have substantial equity that should be charged.
explanation.handLogic: TcTd makes set of Ts. Loses only to 9-J straight (specifically J9; 1 combo blocker-light) and the very rare T8/T4 two-pair combos in BTN flop call range. Beats every overpair, every Tx, every weaker pair, every two-pair, every draw.
explanation.sizingLogic: Big check-raise charges 9-out FDs (~25% equity), 8-out OESDs, gutshots, plus weaker made hands like overpairs/Tx that may stack off. Small check-raise also defensible but surrenders value-extraction vs draws that would call big anyway. Call slowplays but lets draws realize free river equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top set on draw-intensifier turns when draws are dense in villain range surrenders the maximum value-extraction window.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on draw-heavy turn vs draw-heavy barrel = check-raise BIG (max value from draws).

scenarios.blockerNote: No pair, no draw. Two overcards but the board is low BB-favored and the overcards are of low-prevalence value vs villain range. Spade-suited but no flush draw on rainbow board.

question.prompt: Flop 7s 5d 3h; turn 4c. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Js Ts?

explanation.short: JT-suited no pair on low BB-favored straight-complete turn -- mixed: thin bluff-raise with overcard reach vs straightforward fold both defensible.
explanation.turnLogic: 4c lands the bottom straight via 6. Villain barrel range is polarized (made-straight value vs air). Hero JT has no current equity but BB range advantage on this low board lets a mixed bluff-raise work occasionally; conversely, the made-straight density in villain value range plus zero made-hand backbone keeps fold defensible.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JT-suited on 7-5-3 was thin (two overcards, no pair, no draw, possible backdoor flush via spades). Turn 4 keeps draws dead and shifts villain range polar.
explanation.handLogic: JsTs has no pair, no FD (rainbow board), no straight draw (J-T-7-5-4 needs 8-9 in run-out). Overcard pair outs of J or T do not beat made straights or sets. Pure bluff-or-fold spot with BB range advantage tilting toward mixed.
explanation.sizingLogic: Mixed reflects the strategy tension: small bluff-raise leverages BB range advantage on the polar board; fold respects the thin equity backbone. Population/opponent tendencies tip the choice.
explanation.commonMistake: Always-folding here ignores BB range advantage; always-bluff-raising overestimates fold equity -- mixed reflects the real EV picture.
explanation.takeaway: Naked overcards on low BB-favored polar turn = mixed (real strategy tension).

scenarios.blockerNote: Underpair 88 below Q with no draw. Second-pair (3) on the board does not interact with hero pair.

question.prompt: Flop Qs 7d 3c; turn 3h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with 8d 8c?

explanation.short: 88 underpair on Q-high paired-low turn -- mixed: thin bluff-catch vs fold both defensible.
explanation.turnLogic: 3h pairs the second board card. Villain barrel range polarizes between Qx-trips-or-FH (rare combos via 33/77) and air. Hero 88 is below Q overpair territory and dominated by Qx but ahead of all air and weaker underpairs. The mix tension: bluff-catch the polarized barrel vs accept that 88 is too weak vs the value cluster.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with 88 on Q-7-3 was thin (underpair to Q with no draw). Turn 3 doubles second board card; villain range adds rare trips combos (33/77 only since flop-call kept 33 alive).
explanation.handLogic: 8d8c has underpair to Q, no draw, no relevant blocker. Beats all air bluffs and any 7x-or-down. Loses to all Qx, all overpairs, all turned trip combos.
explanation.sizingLogic: Mixed reflects opponent-and-sizing tension. Small barrel sizing tips toward call (more bluff combos in barrel range); larger sizing tips toward fold (polar value-only). Population tendency to over-bluff small sizings tips toward call slightly.
explanation.commonMistake: Hard-coding either always-fold OR always-call ignores the genuine close decision -- this is a real mix spot.
explanation.takeaway: Underpair on Q-high board-pair-low turn = mixed (sizing-and-opponent dependent thin bluff-catch).

scenarios.blockerNote: Gutshot to 8 makes 7-8-9-T-J. 4 outs to 8. No flush draw despite the multi-FD turn (hero diamonds sit alongside 4d 9d but 7h is the turn so heart FD alive only with hearts in hand which JcTd lacks).

question.prompt: Flop Ah 9d 4d; turn 7h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Jc Td?

explanation.short: JT no pair gutshot on A-high multi-FD turn -- fold; range disadvantage plus gutshot insufficient.
explanation.turnLogic: 7h adds heart-FD threats but hero JT has no hearts. Hero gutshot to 8 only (4 outs ~ 9% one-card). Villain barrel range is Ax-heavy plus draws plus rare bluffs. BB range on A-high boards has fewer Ax combos than BTN preflop range; range disadvantage is structural here.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with JT-offsuit on A-9-4 was thin (overcard J + backdoor straight via the existing 9). Turn 7 lands the gutshot but hero has no live FD.
explanation.handLogic: JcTd has no pair, gutshot to 8 only (4 outs), no FD, no relevant blocker. The 8-out is dominated by villain Ax + made-flush-on-river combos.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases 4 outs given range disadvantage; raising commits without backbone equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Continuing weak gutshots on A-high multi-FD turns when BB range is structurally weak ignores both the equity insufficient and the range-disadvantage.
explanation.takeaway: Naked gutshot on A-high multi-FD turn = fold (range disadvantage + insufficient pot odds).

scenarios.blockerNote: Overpair KK on a board where straight just completed. Hero K-blocker is irrelevant here.

question.prompt: Flop 9s 8d 4c; turn 7h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with Ks Kc?

explanation.short: Overpair KK on BB-favored straight-complete turn -- call (bluff-catch the polarized barrel).
explanation.turnLogic: 7h lands the 5-6-7-8-9 straight. Villain barrel range polarizes: made-straight value (any 6 in hand combinations like A6-suited, T6, 65, 64s) plus air. Hero KK overpair beats every air combo and every weaker pair (TT/JJ/QQ if villain barrels) but loses to made straights. Calling captures the air bucket; raising folds the air bucket immediately.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with KK on 9-8-4 was natural (overpair to entire board, slowplay-flop candidate). Turn 7 polarizes villain barrel.
explanation.handLogic: KsKc has overpair below only AA. Beats every weaker overpair barrel, every Tx, every weaker pair, every air. Loses to A6/T6/65/64-style straights (rare BTN preflop combos) and to AA.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures bluffs and reaches showdown vs polarized barrel. Small check-raise also defensible (charges JJ/TT/9x value while keeping bluffs in) but folds out the air bucket prematurely. Big raise terrible vs straights.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding KK overpair on polar straight turns because of the straight threat over-folds the BB region top.
explanation.takeaway: Overpair KK on BB-favored polar straight-complete turn = call (bluff-catch the polar barrel).

scenarios.blockerNote: A-spade blocks AA combos; on K-8-3-8 board the A-spade is NOT a flush-blocker (board has no flush threats) but reduces villain AA value preflop range.

question.prompt: Flop Kd 8s 3c; turn 8h. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called, BTN now barrels. What is BB's best action with As Td?

explanation.short: AT no pair on K-high board-paired turn -- check-raise small (blocker bluff using A-blocker on counterfeit board).
explanation.turnLogic: 8h doubles up the second board card and counterfeits villain barrel value. Hero AT has zero pair on the board but A-blocker reduces villain AA combos AND the counterfeit dynamic compresses villain Kx region toward calling-not-raising. Small check-raise leverages the polar shift toward villain having either a turned trips combo OR air -- against the air bucket the bluff has good fold equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flop call with AT-offsuit on K-8-3 was thin (overcard A + backdoor straight via Q-J for A-K-Q-J-T). Turn 8 changes nothing for hero hand value but unlocks the polar dynamic for a thin bluff-raise with the A-blocker.
explanation.handLogic: AsTd has no pair, no draw, A-blocker reducing AA combos. Pure bluff-equity story leveraging the counterfeit board structure.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise leverages the counterfeit dynamic + A-blocker. Mixed defensible vs sizing/opponent tendencies. Folding is reasonable too -- but small barrel + A-blocker on this paired structure rewards the bluff-raise in a balanced strategy.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-folding A-high on board-paired turns ignores the A-blocker bluff line that this dynamic supports.
explanation.takeaway: A-high no-draw on board-paired turn = check-raise small (blocker_check_raise on counterfeit dynamic).

scenarios.blockerNote: K-pair with Q kicker; beats every worse Kx and the entire busted-bluff bucket.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 9d 4c; turn 2h; river 7s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called; BTN barrelled turn, BB called; BTN now bets the river. What is BB's best action with Kd Qh?

explanation.short: TPTK on a brick river -- call to bluff-catch the polar third barrel.
explanation.riverLogic: The 7s is a brick: no flush (two spades only), no straight, no pair. Ranges are exactly what they were on the turn. BTN bets a medium river polarized into value Kx-strong / sets / overpairs and busted barrel-bluffs; KQ beats every bluff and every worse Kx.
explanation.rangeContext: After calling flop and turn OOP, BB arrives capped but with plenty of one-pair bluff-catchers. Vs a medium polar bet, MDF (~60%) is filled mostly by top-pair hands; KQ is the top of that class.
explanation.handLogic: KdQh is top pair with the second-best kicker; loses only to AK, K9-two-pair, sets and overpairs. There is no draw equity to think about -- it is purely a showdown call.
explanation.commonMistake: Over-folding TPTK to a third barrel is the single biggest river leak; villain auto-profits bluffing if BB folds its bluff-catchers.
explanation.takeaway: TPTK on a brick river = call; folding over-folds, raising turns a showdown hand into a bluff.

scenarios.blockerNote: Set of nines; holding two of the four 9s reduces villain 9x two-pair combos.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 9d 4c; turn 2h; river 7s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called; BTN barrelled turn, BB called; BTN now bets the river. What is BB's best action with 9c 9h?

explanation.short: Middle set on a brick river -- check-raise small for value.
explanation.riverLogic: The 7s brick leaves BTN value-betting Kx-strong and overpairs that pay a raise. Set of nines beats AK, KQ, K9-two-pair and the lower 44/77 sets; it loses only to the rare KK.
explanation.rangeContext: BB flatted 99 pre, called two streets, and arrives with a hand that crushes the value-betting range. The brick means villain still has thin value (Kx) that calls a small raise.
explanation.handLogic: 9c9h is a set with near-100% equity vs the value range. There is no protection concern on the river -- the raise is pure value extraction.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small check-raise targets Kx value-bets that call one more; a big raise folds out everything but KK and leaves value on the table.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling (slowplay) is acceptable but a small raise extracts more from Kx; auto-folding or over-raising are both wrong.
explanation.takeaway: A set on a brick river = check-raise small for value; size to keep worse Kx in.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 9d 4c; turn 2h; river 7s. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called; BTN barrelled turn, BB called; BTN now bets the river. What is BB's best action with Jh Td?

explanation.short: J-high air on a brick river -- fold; there is no showdown value and no blocker to bluff-raise with.
explanation.riverLogic: The 7s completes nothing. JhTd never paired and has no flush. At the river it cannot beat even a bluff at showdown (both have nothing, but BB checked and faces a bet, so J-high is 0% to be good).
explanation.rangeContext: BB floated two streets with overcards and a backdoor that died. On the river this hand has exactly two options: bluff-raise (needs a blocker and a story) or fold. It holds neither a nut blocker nor a credible value story.
explanation.handLogic: JhTd has no pair, no flush, no straight; it is pure air with no removal of villain value.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling J-high on the river is pure spew -- it can never be good; raising without a blocker has no fold equity vs a polar range.
explanation.takeaway: Busted air with no blocker on the river = fold; never call, only bluff-raise with a blocker.

scenarios.blockerNote: A kicker; blocks AK-better-kicker chops and AA value combos.

question.prompt: Flop Ks 9d 4c; turn 2h; river 7s. BB calls with Ah Kc vs BTN's river barrel. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: Top pair top kicker on a brick river -- the primary reason to call is bluff-catching, not value-raising.
explanation.riverLogic: The 7s brick keeps villain polar. AK beats every busted bluff and every worse Kx; it loses to the small slice of sets / K9-two-pair / overpairs.
explanation.rangeContext: AK is the best one-pair bluff-catcher BB can hold here. Raising it would fold out all the bluffs (which it beats) and only get called by the hands that beat it.
explanation.handLogic: AhKc has top pair, top kicker, and an A-blocker that reduces villain AA. It is a call BECAUSE it catches bluffs -- not because it is raising for value (it is not ahead of the value-betting range enough to raise).
explanation.commonMistake: Mislabelling this a value_raise_river is the error: AK raised here only gets called by better and folds out the bluffs it dominates.
explanation.takeaway: Best one-pair bluff-catcher on a brick = call to bluff-catch (not raise).

question.prompt: Flop Js 8d 5c; turn 3h; river Ac. BTN c-bet small flop, BB called; BTN barrelled turn, BB called; BTN now bets the river. What is BB's best action with Kc Jd?

explanation.short: Pair of jacks after an Ace river vs a big bet -- fold; the river shifted the range to BTN.
